concerns.text: FR-003 mandates including 'digital-technology access' and 'finance access' IN the CSA Index. T022b (Phase 2) and T022 (Phase 4) define the index as 'agronomic only' or 'decoupled' from these variables to avoid multicollinearity. There is NO task that implements the FR-003 requirement to include these variables in the index calculation; the tasks explicitly contradict the spec.

FR-006 explicitly mandates 'Bonferroni correction'. T025 implements 'Bonferroni correction' but the Plan.md (Complexity Tracking) and T025's description text explicitly state 'Benjamini-Hochberg FDR' as a replacement. The task description is contradictory and fails to clearly commit to the mandatory spec requirement (Bonferroni) without ambiguity.

FR-010 requires a 'reduced-batch retry' on timeout. T027 describes this as 'reducing sample size by %' with no specific percentage or logic defined. This makes the requirement untestable and the implementation incomplete for a hard constraint (6-hour limit).

FR-005 requires sampling if raw data > 7GB to achieve N >= 5000. T017 mandates N >= 5000 as a hard target even if data is scarce, creating a potential failure condition not present in the spec's edge case logic ('proceed with available years'). No task handles the 'insufficient data' scenario gracefully as an alternative to the hard target.

T022b defines the CSA Index weighting strategy as 'Inverse Probability Weighting (IPW)'. The spec (FR-003) only requires a 'weighted composite score' without specifying IPW. This introduces a specific statistical methodology not authorized by the spec, creating a scope drift.

FR-007 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping cutoff values. T026 specifies sweeping 'across a broad range in incremental steps' without defining the range or step size. This makes the 'done' state subjective and the testability of FR-007 ambiguous.
